title: 10 best herbal teas for ankylosing spondylitis
intro_desc: Herbal teas for ankylosing spondylitis are a natural remedy that has been found to provide relief from the chronic inflammation and pain associated with the condition. These teas work by reducing inflammation, improving joint mobility, and promoting relaxation. Examples of herbal teas that have shown promise in alleviating symptoms include ginger tea, turmeric tea, and willow bark tea. Drinking these teas regularly can help improve sleep quality, reduce morning stiffness, and increase flexibility, allowing individuals with ankylosing spondylitis to live more comfortably and manage their condition more effectively.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Turmeric teas helps with ankylosing spondylitis because of its potent anti-inflammatory compounds, curcuminoids, which specifically target the inflammatory markers associated with this chronic condition. The tea's bioactive compounds reduce joint pain and stiffness by inhibiting enzymes that cause inflammation, thereby alleviating symptoms such as backache and restricted mobility. Additionally, turmeric's antioxidant properties help to protect against oxidative stress, further supporting the body's natural healing processes and promoting overall well-being.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Curcumin, a bioactive compound found in turmeric, has potent anti-inflammatory effects that can help reduce joint pain and inflammation associated with AS.

Antioxidant activity: Turmeric's antioxidant properties help neutralize free radicals, which can contribute to oxidative stress and tissue damage in patients with AS, potentially slowing disease progression.

Cyclooxygenase inhibition: Curcumin also inhibits the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes like cyclooxygenase-2 (COX-2), which is overexpressed in AS, thereby reducing inflammation and joint damage.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Contain a high concentration of curcuminoids, which have potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, making them effective in reducing joint inflammation and pain associated with ankylosing spondylitis.
Roots: Rich in volatile oils and sesquiterpenes, which have been shown to reduce inflammation and improve mobility in patients with ankylosing spondylitis.
remedy_recipe: Gather high-quality turmeric roots or powdered turmeric of 1500-2000mg.
Boil one cup of filtered water and pour it over the turmeric root or powder.
Steep the mixture for 5-7 minutes allowing the curcumin to infuse into the water.
Strain the tea using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a cup or teapot.
Enjoy the warm tea within 15-20 minutes of preparation, ideally with honey and milk.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ginger teas helps with ankylosing spondylitis because of its anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce joint pain and stiffness. The compounds found in ginger, such as gingerol and shogaol, have been shown to inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines, which are thought to contribute to the development of AS. Drinking herbal ginger teas may also help to relax tense muscles and improve circulation, reducing discomfort and improving overall mobility. Additionally, ginger has been traditionally used to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms associated with joint conditions like arthritis.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Ginger's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as gingerols and shogaols, help reduce inflammation in the joints, which can alleviate pain and stiffness associated with ankylosing spondylitis.
Pain-relieving properties: The analgesic and anti-nociceptive properties of ginger may help relieve chronic back pain and joint pain often experienced by individuals with ankylosing spondylitis.

Antioxidant activity: Ginger's antioxidant properties can help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the body, which can contribute to the development of ankylosing spondylitis and its symptoms.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Rich in compounds like gingerol and shogaol, which have potent anti-inflammatory properties that help alleviate joint pain and stiffness associated with ankylosing spondylitis.
Roots: Contain a unique combination of bioactive compounds, including volatile oils and polyphenols, that work together to reduce inflammation and relieve symptoms of ankylosing spondylitis.
remedy_recipe: Gather fresh or dried ginger root weighing approximately one ounce per quart.
Clean the ginger root by gently scrubbing it under cold running water.
Slice the ginger root into thin pieces and simmer them in boiling water for 10 minutes.
Strain the tea mixture through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a large bowl.
Allow the tea to cool and refrigerate it for at least four hours before serving.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Frankincense teas helps with ankylosing spondylitis because of its potent anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties. The Boswellia serrata extract present in the tea reduces inflammation in the joints, which is a primary symptom of the condition. It also blocks the production of prostaglandins, which are hormones that cause pain and swelling. Additionally, frankincense tea has been shown to improve joint mobility and flexibility, making it an effective natural remedy for managing the symptoms of ankylosing spondylitis and improving overall quality of life.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Frankincense tea's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as boswellic acids and terpenoids, help reduce inflammation in the joints and spine, which is a hallmark symptom of ankylosing spondylitis.

Pain-relieving property: The analgesic and anti-nociceptive properties of frankincense tea can help alleviate chronic pain and stiffness associated with ankylosing spondylitis, allowing for improved mobility and reduced discomfort.

Antioxidant property: Frankincense tea's high antioxidant content helps protect the body from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which is thought to contribute to the development of ankylosing spondylitis.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Rhyzomes contain a high concentration of boswellic acids, which have anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving properties, making them effective in reducing inflammation and joint pain associated with ankylosing spondylitis.

Roots: Roots are rich in sesquiterpenes, including alpha-boswellinic acid, which has been shown to reduce inflammation and improve symptoms of ankylosing spondylitis by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory compounds.
remedy_recipe: Measure one teaspoon of dried boswellia serrata powder and add it to one cup of boiling water.
Allow the mixture to steep for five minutes before straining the tea into a cup.
Add one tablespoon of honey and mix well to create a soothing and flavorful beverage.
Drink the tea immediately and repeat the process three times daily for optimal relief.
Store any leftover boswellia serrata powder in an airtight container for up to six months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: White willow teas helps with ankylosing spondylitis because they contain salicin, a natural pain reliever that targets inflammation in the body. The anti-inflammatory properties of white willow tea can help to reduce joint stiffness and swelling associated with ankylosing spondylitis, providing relief from symptoms such as back pain and limited mobility. Additionally, white willow's ability to reduce inflammation can also help to slow down the progression of joint damage, allowing individuals to maintain a more active lifestyle and improve their overall quality of life.
remedy_properties: Salicin: White willow tea contains salicin, a natural pain reliever and anti-inflammatory compound similar to aspirin. It can help reduce inflammation and alleviate joint pain associated with ankylosing spondylitis.

Antioxidant properties: The antioxidants present in white willow tea may help protect against oxidative stress and damage caused by chronic inflammation, which is often linked to ankylosing spondylitis. This can potentially slow down disease progression.

Analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects: White willow tea has been traditionally used to relieve pain and reduce swelling in joints and muscles. Its analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties may help alleviate the chronic joint pain, stiffness, and discomfort associated with ankylosing spondylitis.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in salicin, a natural anti-inflammatory compound similar to aspirin, which helps reduce joint pain and inflammation associated with ankylosing spondylitis.
Barks: Contain a high concentration of salicylic acid, which has potent anti-inflammatory properties that can help alleviate symptoms such as stiffness and limited mobility.
remedy_recipe: Gather 2 teaspoons of dried salix alba bark and 1 cup of boiling water for steeping.
Combine the salix alba bark with the boiling water in a teapot or infuser for 5-7 minutes of steeping time.
Strain the tea mixture using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a cup or jug to remove solids.
Add honey to taste and stir well to combine, dissolving 1 tablespoon of honey per 8 ounces of tea.
Allow the tea to cool for 10-15 minutes before drinking, allowing the flavors to meld together harmoniously.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: English lavender teas helps with ankylosing spondylitis because of its unique combination of calming and anti-inflammatory properties. remedy_recipe: Gather high-quality dried lavandula angustifolia flowers and boil filtered water in a tea kettle for ten minutes.
Measure one teaspoon of dried lavender flowers per eight ounces of boiling water and add to a teapot or infuser.
Steep the mixture for twelve to fifteen minutes, allowing the lavender essential oils to infuse into the hot water.
Strain the tea into a cup using a tea strainer or infuser and discard the solids after preparation is complete.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Valerian teas helps with ankylosing spondylitis because it promotes relaxation and reduces inflammation, allowing patients to better manage their symptoms. remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried valeriana officinalis roots in autumn, cutting them into smaller pieces and removing any woody parts.
Combine one teaspoon of crushed valeriana officinalis root with 250 milliliters of boiling water, allowing it to steep for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the tea mixture through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a cup, discarding the solids and letting it cool slightly.
Add one tablespoon of honey to taste, if desired, stirring well to combine before drinking the tea immediately.
Prepare a fresh batch every 2-3 days as the valeriana officinalis root loses its potency when stored for extended periods.

Anti-inflammatory compounds: The anti-inflammatory compounds present in slippery elm, such as oligomeric proanthocyanidins (OPCs), may help reduce inflammation and alleviate joint pain associated with AS.

Antioxidants: Slippery elm tea's antioxidants can help protect the body from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to chronic inflammation and tissue degeneration in AS.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the slippery elm plant are rich in mucilage, a gel-like substance that can help soothe and protect the inflamed joints and spine associated with ankylosing spondylitis.
Barks: Slippery elm barks contain anti-inflammatory compounds that may help reduce joint pain and stiffness in individuals with ankylosing spondylitis.
remedy_recipe: Gather one teaspoon of dried ulmus rubra bark or powder and three cups of boiling water.
Steep the ulmus rubra mixture in hot water for ten minutes to allow for optimal extraction.
Strain the tea leaves using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth to remove any solids.
Mix one teaspoon of honey with the cooled tea to enhance flavor and soothe inflammation.
Drink the tea twice daily, one hour before meals, for at least six weeks to monitor progress.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Red clover teas helps with ankylosing spondylitis because it contains isoflavones, a type of phytoestrogen that has anti-inflammatory properties. These isoflavones help to reduce the production of pro-inflammatory compounds in the body, which can alleviate symptoms such as pain and stiffness associated with ankylosing spondylitis. Additionally, red clover tea's flavonoids have been shown to modulate the immune response and improve joint health by reducing oxidative stress and inflammation, making it a potential complementary therapy for managing this chronic condition.
remedy_properties: Isoflavones: Red clover tea contains isoflavones, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, helping to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the body, key factors contributing to AS.

Phytoestrogens: The phytoestrogens in red clover tea may help alleviate symptoms of AS by modulating estrogen receptors, potentially reducing inflammation and joint pain, as well as improving bone density and overall joint health.

Flavonoids: Red clover tea is rich in flavonoids, which have potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, helping to reduce oxidative stress, improve blood flow, and alleviate symptoms of AS such as joint stiffness and pain.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots contain triterpenoids, which have anti-inflammatory properties that help alleviate joint pain and swelling associated with ankylosing spondylitis.
Flowers: The flowers are rich in isoflavones, which have been shown to reduce inflammation and improve symptoms of ankylosing spondylitis by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality red clover flowers in full bloom ensuring correct identification and cleanliness.
Dry the freshly harvested red clover flowers using a food dehydrator on low heat for eight hours.
Combine one teaspoon of dried red clover flowers with boiling water in a teapot steeping for ten to fifteen minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup using a tea strainer or cheesecloth discarding the solids immediately.
Allow the tea to cool slightly before consuming one to three cups per day as needed.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat ankylosing spondylitis with herbal teas is by brewing a soothing blend of turmeric, ginger, and willow bark. Turmeric's curcumin reduces inflammation, while ginger alleviates pain and stiffness. Willow bark's salicin has anti-inflammatory properties similar to aspirin. Drinking this tea three times a day can help alleviate symptoms, promote relaxation, and improve joint mobility.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to ankylosing spondylitis that are treated with herbal teas are those that share similar symptoms such as inflammation, stiffness, and joint pain. Examples include fibromyalgia, rheumatoid arthritis, gout, and osteoarthritis. Herbal teas like turmeric, ginger, and ashwagandha have anti-inflammatory properties that can help alleviate these symptoms. Other herbal teas used to treat similar conditions include devil's claw, willow bark, and stinging nettle tea, which are believed to reduce pain and inflammation.
